1	/// JSON5 Token-Based Scanner/Parser
2	///
3	/// Parses JSON5 text into Expr AST values. JSON5 is a superset of JSON
4	/// based on ECMAScript 5.1 that supports comments, trailing commas,
5	/// unquoted keys, single-quoted strings, hex numbers, Infinity, NaN, etc.
6	///
7	/// Architecture: a scanner reads source bytes and produces typed tokens;
8	/// the parser only consumes tokens and never touches source/pos directly.
9	///
10	/// Reference: https://spec.json5.org/
11	pub const JSON5Parser = struct {
12	    source: []const u8,
13	    pos: usize,
14	    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
15	    stack_check: bun.StackCheck,
16	    token: Token,
17	
18	    const Token = struct {
19	        loc: logger.Loc,
20	        data: Data,
21	
22	        const Data = union(enum) {
23	            eof,
24	            // Structural
25	            left_brace,
26	            right_brace,
27	            left_bracket,
28	            right_bracket,
29	            colon,
30	            comma,
31	            // Values
32	            string: []u8,
33	            number: f64,
34	            boolean: bool,
35	            null,
36	            // Identifiers (for unquoted keys that aren't keywords)
37	            identifier: []u8,
38	
39	            fn canStartValue(data: Data) bool {
40	                return switch (data) {
41	                    .string, .number, .boolean, .identifier, .null, .left_brace, .left_bracket => true,
42	                    .eof, .right_brace, .right_bracket, .colon, .comma => false,
43	                };
44	            }
45	        };
46	    };
47	
48	    const ParseError = OOM || error{
49	        UnexpectedCharacter,
50	        UnexpectedToken,
51	        UnexpectedEof,
52	        UnterminatedString,
53	        UnterminatedComment,
54	        UnterminatedObject,
55	        UnterminatedArray,
56	        UnterminatedEscape,
57	        InvalidNumber,
58	        LeadingZeros,
59	        InvalidHexNumber,
60	        InvalidHexEscape,
61	        InvalidUnicodeEscape,
62	        OctalEscape,
63	        ExpectedColon,
64	        ExpectedComma,
65	        ExpectedClosingBrace,
66	        ExpectedClosingBracket,
67	        InvalidIdentifier,
68	        TrailingData,
69	        StackOverflow,
70	    };
71	
72	    pub const Error = union(enum) {
73	        oom,
74	        stack_overflow,
75	        unexpected_character: struct { pos: usize },
76	        unexpected_token: struct { pos: usize },
77	        unexpected_eof: struct { pos: usize },
78	        unterminated_string: struct { pos: usize },
79	        unterminated_comment: struct { pos: usize },
80	        unterminated_object: struct { pos: usize },
81	        unterminated_array: struct { pos: usize },
82	        unterminated_escape: struct { pos: usize },
83	        invalid_number: struct { pos: usize },
84	        leading_zeros: struct { pos: usize },
85	        invalid_hex_number: struct { pos: usize },
86	        invalid_hex_escape: struct { pos: usize },
87	        invalid_unicode_escape: struct { pos: usize },
88	        octal_escape: struct { pos: usize },
89	        expected_colon: struct { pos: usize },
90	        expected_comma: struct { pos: usize },
91	        expected_closing_brace: struct { pos: usize },
92	        expected_closing_bracket: struct { pos: usize },
93	        invalid_identifier: struct { pos: usize },
94	        trailing_data: struct { pos: usize },
95	
96	        pub fn addToLog(this: *const Error, source: *const logger.Source, log: *logger.Log) (OOM || error{StackOverflow})!void {
97	            const loc: logger.Loc = switch (this.*) {
98	                .oom => return error.OutOfMemory,
99	                .stack_overflow => return error.StackOverflow,
100	                inline else => |e| .{ .start = @intCast(e.pos) },
101	            };
102	            const msg: []const u8 = switch (this.*) {
103	                .oom, .stack_overflow => unreachable,
104	                .unexpected_character => "Unexpected character",
105	                .unexpected_token => "Unexpected token",
106	                .unexpected_eof => "Unexpected end of input",
107	                .unterminated_string => "Unterminated string",
108	                .unterminated_comment => "Unterminated multi-line comment",
109	                .unterminated_object => "Unterminated object",
110	                .unterminated_array => "Unterminated array",
111	                .unterminated_escape => "Unexpected end of input in escape sequence",
112	                .invalid_number => "Invalid number",
113	                .leading_zeros => "Leading zeros are not allowed in JSON5",
114	                .invalid_hex_number => "Invalid hex number",
115	                .invalid_hex_escape => "Invalid hex escape",
116	                .invalid_unicode_escape => "Invalid unicode escape: expected 4 hex digits",
117	                .octal_escape => "Octal escape sequences are not allowed in JSON5",
118	                .expected_colon => "Expected ':' after object key",
119	                .expected_comma => "Expected ','",
120	                .expected_closing_brace => "Expected '}'",
121	                .expected_closing_bracket => "Expected ']'",
122	                .invalid_identifier => "Invalid identifier start character",
123	                .trailing_data => "Unexpected token after JSON5 value",
124	            };
125	            try log.addError(source, loc, msg);
126	        }
127	    };

129	    fn toError(err: ParseError, parser: *const JSON5Parser) Error {
130	        const token_pos = parser.token.loc.toUsize();
131	        const scan_pos = parser.pos;
132	        return switch (err) {
133	            error.OutOfMemory => .oom,
134	            error.StackOverflow => .stack_overflow,
135	            // Scanner errors use scan position
136	            error.UnexpectedCharacter => .{ .unexpected_character = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
137	            error.UnterminatedString => .{ .unterminated_string = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
138	            error.UnterminatedComment => .{ .unterminated_comment = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
139	            error.UnterminatedEscape => .{ .unterminated_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
140	            error.InvalidNumber => .{ .invalid_number = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
141	            error.LeadingZeros => .{ .leading_zeros = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
142	            error.InvalidHexNumber => .{ .invalid_hex_number = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
143	            error.InvalidHexEscape => .{ .invalid_hex_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
144	            error.InvalidUnicodeEscape => .{ .invalid_unicode_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
145	            error.OctalEscape => .{ .octal_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
146	            error.InvalidIdentifier => .{ .invalid_identifier = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
147	            // Parser errors use token position
148	            error.UnexpectedToken => .{ .unexpected_token = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
149	            error.UnexpectedEof => if (parser.token.data == .eof)
150	                .{ .unexpected_eof = .{ .pos = token_pos } }
151	            else
152	                .{ .unexpected_token = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
153	            error.TrailingData => .{ .trailing_data = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
154	            error.ExpectedColon => .{ .expected_colon = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
155	            error.UnterminatedObject => .{ .unterminated_object = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
156	            error.ExpectedComma => .{ .expected_comma = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
157	            error.ExpectedClosingBrace => .{ .expected_closing_brace = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
158	            error.UnterminatedArray => .{ .unterminated_array = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
159	            error.ExpectedClosingBracket => .{ .expected_closing_bracket = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
160	        };
161	    }

163	    const ExternalError = OOM || error{ SyntaxError, StackOverflow };

165	    pub fn parse(source: *const logger.Source, log: *logger.Log, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ExternalError!Expr {
166	        var parser = JSON5Parser{
167	            .source = source.contents,
168	            .pos = 0,
169	            .allocator = allocator,
170	            .stack_check = .init(),
171	            .token = .{ .loc = .{}, .data = .eof },
172	        };
173	        const result = parser.parseRoot() catch |err| {
174	            const e = toError(err, &parser);
175	            try e.addToLog(source, log);
176	            return error.SyntaxError;
177	        };
178	        return result;
179	    }

186	        if (self.pos < self.source.len) return self.source[self.pos];
187	        return 0;
188	    }

191	        self.token.data = next: switch (self.peek()) {

194	                break :next .eof;
195	            },

198	                self.pos += 1;

## Comprehensive Report: Bun's INI Parsing Infrastructure

Based on my thorough exploration of the Bun codebase, here are all the findings regarding INI parsing and related format infrastructure:

### 1. INI Parser Implementation

**File:** `/src/ini.zig` (16,803 tokens)

The INI parser is fully implemented in Zig with comprehensive features:

- **Main Parser Structure:** `Parser` struct with:
  - `init()`, `deinit()`, and `parse()` methods
  - Support for sections: `[foo]`, `[a.b.c]` for nested properties
  - Key-value pairs with various value types (strings, booleans, null, undefined)
  - Array support with `key[] = value` syntax
  - Environment variable substitution: `${VAR}` and `${VAR?}` (optional)
  - Escape sequence handling for special characters
  - Comment support: `;` and `#` delimiters
  - JSON parsing for values in quotes
  - Support for escaped sections like `[x\.y\.z]` (literal dots)

- **Environment Variable Features:**
  - `${VAR}` - replaces with environment variable or leaves as-is
  - `${VAR?}` - optional: replaces with env var or empty string
  - Escape handling: `\${VAR}` prevents substitution
  - Double escaping: `\\${VAR}` results in `\value`

- **Special Behaviors (npm/ini compatibility):**
  - Handles edge cases like setting a key before a section with same name
  - Array handling with duplicate keys
  - JSON value parsing for quoted values
  - Weird section names like `"[disturbing]"` as a quoted key

- **Testing API:** `IniTestingAPIs` struct with two public functions:
  - `parse(globalThis, callframe)` - exposed to JS via `bun:internal-for-testing`
  - `loadNpmrcFromJS(globalThis, callframe)` - loads and parses .npmrc files with environment variable handling

### 2. INI Testing

**File:** `/test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`

Comprehensive test suite covering:
- Basic parsing (sections, key-value pairs)
- Environment variable substitution (40+ test cases covering escaping, optional vars, quoting)
- Unicode support (emoji and special characters)
- Duplicate properties and array handling
- Edge cases (truncated UTF-8 sequences, invalid inputs)
- Actual .npmrc parsing integration test using `Bun.$` commands
- Test data file: `/test/js/bun/ini/foo.ini` (complex fixture with nested structures)

### 3. Internal Testing Module

**File:** `/src/js/internal-for-testing.ts` (lines 49-61)

INI internals are exposed for testing:

```typescript
export const iniInternals = {
  parse: $newZigFunction("ini.zig", "IniTestingAPIs.parse", 1),
  loadNpmrc: $newZigFunction("ini.zig", "IniTestingAPIs.loadNpmrcFromJS", 2),
};
```

Usage in tests: `const { iniInternals } = require("bun:internal-for-testing");`

### 4. Format Objects on Bun Global

**File:** `/src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` (lines 66-70, 1273-1289)

The Bun object exposes format parsers via lazy-loaded properties:

```zig
pub const JSONC = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getJSONCObject);
pub const markdown = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getMarkdownObject);
pub const TOML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getTOMLObject);
pub const JSON5 = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getJSON5Object);
pub const YAML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getYAMLObject);
```

These are lazy-loaded (initialized on first access) for performance.

### 5. Related Format APIs

The Bun object provides a consistent API pattern for all formats:

#### **JSON5** (`/src/bun.js/api/JSON5Object.zig`)
- Methods: `parse(string)` and `stringify(value, replacer?, space?)`
- Supports comments, trailing commas, unquoted keys, single quotes

#### **TOML** (`/src/bun.js/api/TOMLObject.zig`)
- Methods: `parse(string)` only
- Full TOML 1.0 support including inline tables, table arrays, nested tables

#### **JSONC** (`/src/bun.js/api/JSONCObject.zig`)
- Methods: `parse(string)` only
- JSON with C-style comments, used for TypeScript config files

#### **YAML** (`/src/bun.js/api/YAMLObject.zig`)
- Methods: `parse(string/buffer)` and `stringify(value, replacer?, space?)`
- Full YAML support with anchors and aliases

#### **Testing Pattern:**
All format objects follow the same initialization pattern:
1. Create empty object with JSValue
2. Add `parse` and/or `stringify` methods via `JSFunction.create()`
3. Return the object

### 6. Directory Structure

**Source Code Layout:**

```
/src/
  ini.zig                          # INI parser (NOT in interchange/)
  /bun.js/
    api/
      BunObject.zig               # Bun global object definition
      JSONCObject.zig             # JSONC format
      JSON5Object.zig             # JSON5 format
      TOMLObject.zig              # TOML format
      YAMLObject.zig              # YAML format
    bindings/
      ZigGlobalObject.h/cpp       # Global object C++ bindings
      BunObject.cpp               # C++ implementation details
  /interchange/
    json.zig                       # JSON parser
    json5.zig                      # JSON5 interchange format
    toml.zig                       # TOML interchange format
    yaml.zig                       # YAML interchange format
  /js/
    internal-for-testing.ts       # Testing APIs
    /builtins/                    # JS builtin functions
    /internal/                    # Internal JS modules
    /node/                        # Node.js compatibility
    /bun/                         # Bun-specific modules
    /thirdparty/                  # NPM replacements (ws, fetch, etc.)
```

**Test Layout:**

```
/test/js/bun/
  /ini/
    ini.test.ts                   # Comprehensive INI parser tests
    foo.ini                       # Test fixture
  /json5/
    json5-test-suite.test.ts     # JSON5 tests
  /resolve/
    /toml/
      toml.test.js               # TOML tests
    /yaml/
      yaml.test.js               # YAML tests
  /yaml/
    yaml.test.ts                 # YAML comprehensive tests
    yaml-test-suite.test.ts      # YAML suite tests
```

### 7. Global Object Registration

**File:** `/src/bun.js/bindings/ZigGlobalObject.h/cpp`

The ZigGlobalObject is where the Bun global and its properties are defined. All format objects are registered as lazy properties through the BunObject table in C++ (see `BunObject+exports.h`).

### 8. Key API Patterns

**Consistent for all parsers:**

```typescript
// TOML
Bun.TOML.parse(string)

// JSON5
Bun.JSON5.parse(string)
Bun.JSON5.stringify(value, replacer?, space?)

// YAML
Bun.YAML.parse(string | Buffer)
Bun.YAML.stringify(value, replacer?, space?)

// JSONC
Bun.JSONC.parse(string)

// INI (internal testing only)
const { iniInternals } = require("bun:internal-for-testing");
iniInternals.parse(string)
iniInternals.loadNpmrc(string, env?)
```

### 9. Important Notes on INI

1. **INI is NOT exposed on public Bun API** - it's only available via `bun:internal-for-testing`
2. **Used internally for .npmrc parsing** - The `loadNpmrcFromJS` function is specifically for npm configuration
3. **Environment variable support is built-in** - The parser handles env var substitution natively
4. **Follows npm/ini behavior** - Compatible with the npm package's INI parser behavior
5. **No public entry point** - Unlike TOML, JSON5, YAML, JSONC, there's no `Bun.INI` object

### 10. Zig-to-JavaScript Binding Pattern

All format objects use the same binding strategy:

```zig
// In BunObject.zig:
pub fn getTOMLObject(globalThis: *jsc.JSGlobalObject, _: *jsc.JSObject) jsc.JSValue {
    return TOMLObject.create(globalThis);
}

// Exported as lazy property:
pub const TOML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getTOMLObject);

// In exportAll():
@export(&BunObject.TOML, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("TOML") });

// C++ integration:
// BunObject.cpp references these via generated bindings
// ZigGlobalObject registers them in the global object
```

This design allows:
- Lazy initialization (only created when accessed)
- Type-safe Zig implementation
- JavaScript-friendly object API
- Memory efficiency

## Comprehensive Analysis: Bun's Format Namespace Implementation (TOML, JSONC, JSON5, YAML)

I've thoroughly explored the complete end-to-end implementation of Bun's format namespaces. Here's the complete pipeline:

### 1. **JavaScript/TypeScript Entry Layer**

Users call the global Bun object methods:
```typescript
Bun.TOML.parse("...")
Bun.JSONC.parse("...")
Bun.JSON5.parse("...") or Bun.JSON5.stringify(...)
Bun.YAML.parse("...") or Bun.YAML.stringify(...)
```

### 2. **JavaScript Binding Layer** (Zig - Exposes to JavaScript)

Four separate Zig files implement the JavaScript API objects:

#### **2a. TOML Object** (`src/bun.js/api/TOMLObject.zig`)
- **create()** function: Creates a JS object with one property: `parse` (arity 1)
- **parse()** handler: Takes a string argument, returns parsed JavaScript object
- No stringify method (TOML is parse-only)

#### **2b. JSON5 Object** (`src/bun.js/api/JSON5Object.zig`)
- **create()** function: Creates a JS object with two properties: `parse` (arity 1) and `stringify` (arity 3)
- **parse()** handler: Takes a string, returns parsed object
- **stringify()** handler: Takes value, replacer (unsupported), space parameter
- Includes custom `Stringifier` struct for JSON5 serialization with:
  - Circular reference detection
  - Single-quoted strings
  - Unquoted object keys (when valid identifiers)
  - Trailing comma support
  - Spacing control (minified/number/string)

#### **2c. JSONC Object** (`src/bun.js/api/JSONCObject.zig`)
- **create()** function: Creates a JS object with one property: `parse` (arity 1)
- **parse()** handler: Calls `json.parseTSConfig()` with `force_utf8=true`
- No stringify (JSONC is parse-only)

#### **2d. YAML Object** (`src/bun.js/api/YAMLObject.zig`)
- **create()** function: Creates a JS object with two properties: `parse` (arity 1) and `stringify` (arity 3)
- **parse()** handler: Takes various input types (string, Buffer, Blob, typed arrays)
- **stringify()** handler: Full YAML serialization with:
  - Anchor/alias generation for repeated objects
  - Block and flow style support
  - YAML-safe string quoting
  - Indentation control
- Includes complex `Stringifier` struct with reference tracking

### 3. **Core Parsing Engines** (Zig - Interchange Layer)

Files in `src/interchange/`:

#### **3a. TOML Parser** (`src/interchange/toml.zig`)
- **TOML struct** with:
  - **parse()**: Entry point, returns AST Expr
  - **runParser()**: Main parsing logic handling:
    - Child tables (`[table]`)
    - Table arrays (`[[array]]`)
    - Key-value assignments
  - **parseAssignment()**: Handles dotted keys and array declarations
  - **parseValue()**: Recursive value parser for:
    - Booleans, strings, numbers
    - Objects, arrays
    - Inline tables and arrays
  - **parseKey()**: Handles dotted notation keys
  - **parseMaybeTrailingComma()**: Supports trailing commas
- **Lexer dependency**: Uses custom TOML lexer (`src/interchange/toml/lexer.zig`)
- **Output**: Returns `Expr` (AST expression) in bun.ast format

#### **3b. JSON5 Parser** (`src/interchange/json5.zig`)
- **JSON5Parser struct** with:
  - **parse()**: Entry point, returns AST Expr
  - **scan()**: Token scanner (consumes source bytes)
  - **Token data structure** with enum variants:
    - Structural: `{`, `}`, `[`, `]`, `:`, `,`
    - Values: `string`, `number`, `boolean`, `null`
    - Identifiers (for unquoted keys)
  - **parseRoot()**: Main parsing for objects/arrays
  - Full **error handling** with 16+ error types
  - Supports:
    - Comments (line and block)
    - Trailing commas
    - Unquoted keys
    - Single-quoted strings
    - Hex numbers, Infinity, NaN
    - Escape sequences
- **Output**: Returns `Expr` (AST expression)

#### **3c. JSONC/JSON Parser** (`src/interchange/json.zig`)
- **parseTSConfig()**: Main entry point for JSONC (TypeScript config)
  - Creates `TSConfigParser = JSONLikeParser` with options:
    - `allow_comments = true`
    - `allow_trailing_commas = true`
    - `is_json = true`
  - Uses `JSONLikeParser` generic over lexer options
  - Supports comments, trailing commas
- **JSONLikeParser**: Generic parser over JSON lexer options
  - Uses `js_lexer` with customizable JSONOptions
- **Output**: Returns `Expr` (AST expression)

#### **3d. YAML Parser** (`src/interchange/yaml.zig`)
- **YAML struct** with:
  - **parse()**: Entry point
  - Returns arrays for multi-document streams
  - Single document returns root Expr
- Complex **Parser(encoding)** generic struct for UTF-8/UTF-16
  - Handles block, flow, and key contexts
  - Indentation tracking
  - Chomp indicators (strip/clip/keep trailing newlines)
  - Anchor/alias support (YAML anchors: `&name`, aliases: `*name`)
- **Output**: Returns `Expr` (AST expression) or array of Expr for multi-doc

### 4. **AST Representation Layer**

All parsers output `Expr` from `bun.ast` (AST module):
```zig
Expr = struct {
    loc: logger.Loc,
    data: Data
}

Data = union(enum) {
    e_null,
    e_boolean: { value: bool }
    e_number: { value: f64 }
    e_string: E.String
    e_array: *E.Array
    e_object: *E.Object
    ...
}
```

This is the **intermediate representation** that bridges Zig parsing to JavaScript.

### 5. **AST-to-JavaScript Conversion**

Each object file (TOMLObject, JSON5Object, etc.) converts AST back to JS:

**TOMLObject.parse()**:
```zig
// 1. Parse to AST Expr
const parse_result = TOML.parse(source, &log, allocator, false)

// 2. Print AST as JSON (buffer_writer + js_printer.BufferPrinter)
js_printer.printJSON(..., parse_result, ...)

// 3. Parse resulting JSON string
out.toJSByParseJSON(globalThis)
```

**JSON5Object.parse()**:
```zig
// 1. Parse to AST Expr
const root = json5.JSON5Parser.parse(source, &log, allocator)

// 2. Convert AST to JS recursively (exprToJS function)
exprToJS(root, global) {
    switch expr.data:
        e_null => return .null
        e_boolean => return .jsBoolean(...)
        e_number => return .jsNumber(...)
        e_string => return str.toJS(...)
        e_array => create JS array, recursively convert items
        e_object => create JS object, recursively convert properties
}
```

**YAMLObject.parse()**:
```zig
// 1. Parse to AST Expr
const root = bun.interchange.yaml.YAML.parse(source, &log, allocator)

// 2. Convert via ParserCtx.toJS() (similar to JSON5)
```

### 6. **Global Registration** (BunObject.zig)

`src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` orchestrates the namespace exposure:

```zig
pub const BunObject = struct {
    // Lazy property callbacks - created on-demand
    pub const JSONC = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getJSONCObject);
    pub const TOML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getTOMLObject);
    pub const JSON5 = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getJSON5Object);
    pub const YAML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getYAMLObject);
    
    // ... exports in separate section ...
    @export(&BunObject.JSONC, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("JSONC") });
    @export(&BunObject.TOML, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("TOML") });
    @export(&BunObject.JSON5, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("JSON5") });
    @export(&BunObject.YAML, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("YAML") });
}

// Getter implementations:
pub fn getJSONCObject(globalThis, _) { return JSONCObject.create(globalThis); }
pub fn getTOMLObject(globalThis, _) { return TOMLObject.create(globalThis); }
pub fn getJSON5Object(globalThis, _) { return JSON5Object.create(globalThis); }
pub fn getYAMLObject(globalThis, _) { return YAMLObject.create(globalThis); }
```

### 7. **Module Imports** (api.zig)

`src/bun.js/api.zig` imports all format objects:
```zig
pub const JSONCObject = @import("./api/JSONCObject.zig");
pub const TOMLObject = @import("./api/TOMLObject.zig");
pub const JSON5Object = @import("./api/JSON5Object.zig");
pub const YAMLObject = @import("./api/YAMLObject.zig");
```

### 8. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/src/interchange.zig` | Entry point exporting json, json5, toml, yaml modules |
| `/src/interchange/toml.zig` | TOML parser (393 lines) |
| `/src/interchange/json5.zig` | JSON5 parser with token-based scanner |
| `/src/interchange/json.zig` | JSON/JSONC parser (uses TSConfigParser) |
| `/src/interchange/yaml.zig` | YAML parser (50k+ lines) with printer |
| `/src/interchange/toml/lexer.zig` | TOML lexer for tokenization |
| `/src/bun.js/api/TOMLObject.zig` | JS binding for Bun.TOML |
| `/src/bun.js/api/JSON5Object.zig` | JS binding for Bun.JSON5 |
| `/src/bun.js/api/JSONCObject.zig` | JS binding for Bun.JSONC |
| `/src/bun.js/api/YAMLObject.zig` | JS binding for Bun.YAML |
| `/src/bun.js/api.zig` | Module imports |
| `/src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` | Global registration (2098 lines) |
| `/test/js/bun/jsonc/jsonc.test.ts` | JSONC tests |
| `/test/js/bun/yaml/yaml.test.ts` | YAML tests |
| `/test/js/bun/resolve/toml/toml.test.js` | TOML tests |

### 9. **Complete Call Flow Example**

```
User: Bun.TOML.parse(tomlString)
  ↓
BunObject.zig: getTOMLObject() [lazy init]
  ↓
TOMLObject.zig: create() → returns JS object with parse function
  ↓
User calls parse(tomlString)
  ↓
TOMLObject.zig: parse() handler
  ├─ Create arena allocator
  ├─ Create logger for errors
  └─ Call TOML.parse(source, &log, allocator, false)
  ↓
toml.zig: TOML.parse()
  ├─ Initialize TOML parser with lexer
  └─ Call runParser() → returns Expr AST
  ↓
TOMLObject.zig: Convert AST to JSON
  ├─ Use js_printer.printJSON() on Expr
  ├─ Serialize to JSON string
  └─ toJSByParseJSON() → JS object
  ↓
User receives: { ... parsed TOML as JS object ... }
```

### 10. **Stringify Pipelines** (JSON5 & YAML only)

**JSON5.stringify()**:
- Creates `Stringifier` struct
- Detects circular references
- Recursively stringifies with:
  - Unquoted keys (if valid identifiers)
  - Single-quoted strings
  - Trailing commas in arrays/objects
- Returns JS string

**YAML.stringify()**:
- Two-pass algorithm:
  1. `findAnchorsAndAliases()` - scans for repeated objects
  2. `stringify()` - generates YAML with anchors (`&name`) and aliases (`*name`)
- Handles indentation, chomp, block/flow styles
- Returns JS string

All files use **absolute paths** as documented in the codebase exploration.

Each object file (TOMLObject, JSON5Object, etc.) converts AST back to JS:

**TOMLObject.parse()**:
```zig
// 1. Parse to AST Expr
const parse_result = TOML.parse(source, &log, allocator, false)

// 2. Print AST as JSON (buffer_writer + js_printer.BufferPrinter)
js_printer.printJSON(..., parse_result, ...)

// 3. Parse resulting JSON string
out.toJSByParseJSON(globalThis)
```

**JSON5Object.parse()**:
```zig
// 1. Parse to AST Expr
const root = json5.JSON5Parser.parse(source, &log, allocator)

// 2. Convert AST to JS recursively (exprToJS function)
exprToJS(root, global) {
    switch expr.data:
        e_null => return .null
        e_boolean => return .jsBoolean(...)
        e_number => return .jsNumber(...)
        e_string => return str.toJS(...)
        e_array => create JS array, recursively convert items
        e_object => create JS object, recursively convert properties
}
```

**YAMLObject.parse()**:
```zig
// 1. Parse to AST Expr
const root = bun.interchange.yaml.YAML.parse(source, &log, allocator)

// 2. Convert via ParserCtx.toJS() (similar to JSON5)
```

### 6. **Global Registration** (BunObject.zig)

`src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` orchestrates the namespace exposure:

```zig
pub const BunObject = struct {
    // Lazy property callbacks - created on-demand
    pub const JSONC = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getJSONCObject);
    pub const TOML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getTOMLObject);
    pub const JSON5 = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getJSON5Object);
    pub const YAML = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getYAMLObject);
    
    // ... exports in separate section ...
    @export(&BunObject.JSONC, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("JSONC") });
    @export(&BunObject.TOML, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("TOML") });
    @export(&BunObject.JSON5, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("JSON5") });
    @export(&BunObject.YAML, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("YAML") });
}

// Getter implementations:
pub fn getJSONCObject(globalThis, _) { return JSONCObject.create(globalThis); }
pub fn getTOMLObject(globalThis, _) { return TOMLObject.create(globalThis); }
pub fn getJSON5Object(globalThis, _) { return JSON5Object.create(globalThis); }
pub fn getYAMLObject(globalThis, _) { return YAMLObject.create(globalThis); }
```

### 7. **Module Imports** (api.zig)

`src/bun.js/api.zig` imports all format objects:
```zig
pub const JSONCObject = @import("./api/JSONCObject.zig");
pub const TOMLObject = @import("./api/TOMLObject.zig");
pub const JSON5Object = @import("./api/JSON5Object.zig");
pub const YAMLObject = @import("./api/YAMLObject.zig");
```

### 8. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/src/interchange.zig` | Entry point exporting json, json5, toml, yaml modules |
| `/src/interchange/toml.zig` | TOML parser (393 lines) |
| `/src/interchange/json5.zig` | JSON5 parser with token-based scanner |
| `/src/interchange/json.zig` | JSON/JSONC parser (uses TSConfigParser) |
| `/src/interchange/yaml.zig` | YAML parser (50k+ lines) with printer |
| `/src/interchange/toml/lexer.zig` | TOML lexer for tokenization |
| `/src/bun.js/api/TOMLObject.zig` | JS binding for Bun.TOML |
| `/src/bun.js/api/JSON5Object.zig` | JS binding for Bun.JSON5 |
| `/src/bun.js/api/JSONCObject.zig` | JS binding for Bun.JSONC |
| `/src/bun.js/api/YAMLObject.zig` | JS binding for Bun.YAML |
| `/src/bun.js/api.zig` | Module imports |
| `/src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` | Global registration (2098 lines) |
| `/test/js/bun/jsonc/jsonc.test.ts` | JSONC tests |
| `/test/js/bun/yaml/yaml.test.ts` | YAML tests |
| `/test/js/bun/resolve/toml/toml.test.js` | TOML tests |

### 9. **Complete Call Flow Example**

```
User: Bun.TOML.parse(tomlString)
  ↓
BunObject.zig: getTOMLObject() [lazy init]
  ↓
TOMLObject.zig: create() → returns JS object with parse function
  ↓
User calls parse(tomlString)
  ↓
TOMLObject.zig: parse() handler
  ├─ Create arena allocator
  ├─ Create logger for errors
  └─ Call TOML.parse(source, &log, allocator, false)
  ↓
toml.zig: TOML.parse()
  ├─ Initialize TOML parser with lexer
  └─ Call runParser() → returns Expr AST
  ↓
TOMLObject.zig: Convert AST to JSON
  ├─ Use js_printer.printJSON() on Expr
  ├─ Serialize to JSON string
  └─ toJSByParseJSON() → JS object
  ↓
User receives: { ... parsed TOML as JS object ... }
```

### 10. **Stringify Pipelines** (JSON5 & YAML only)

**JSON5.stringify()**:
- Creates `Stringifier` struct
- Detects circular references
- Recursively stringifies with:
  - Unquoted keys (if valid identifiers)
  - Single-quoted strings
  - Trailing commas in arrays/objects
- Returns JS string

**YAML.stringify()**:
- Two-pass algorithm:
  1. `findAnchorsAndAliases()` - scans for repeated objects
  2. `stringify()` - generates YAML with anchors (`&name`) and aliases (`*name`)
- Handles indentation, chomp, block/flow styles
- Returns JS string

All files use **absolute paths** as documented in the codebase exploration.

input.content:
# Plan: Add `Bun.INI` Namespace with `parse` and `stringify`

## Context

Bun already exposes `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML` as
public format-namespace objects. There is an existing *internal* INI parser
(`src/ini.zig`) used only for `.npmrc` processing — it is tied to npm-specific
semantics (env-var substitution, `key[] = value` arrays, `DotEnv.Loader`).

The goal is to expose a new, **self-contained** `Bun.INI` namespace following
the exact same pattern as the existing format objects. The parser should handle
the standard subset: sections → nested objects, `key = value` pairs, and
`;`/`#` comment lines. A stringify method is required too.

**Do NOT import or re-use `src/ini.zig`** — it is the internal npm parser.

---

## Architecture Overview

Every existing format namespace wires through exactly these layers:

```
src/interchange/X.zig            ← standalone parse engine (Expr AST output)
src/interchange.zig              ← re-exports every interchange module
src/bun.js/api/XObject.zig       ← JSC binding (create/parse/stringify)
src/bun.js/api.zig               ← imports XObject
src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig     ← lazy prop + getter fn + @export
src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h  ← FOR_EACH_GETTER macro
src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp        ← bunObjectTable entry
test/js/bun/ini/ini-public.test.ts       ← tests
```

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — `src/interchange/ini.zig` (new file)

Create a self-contained INI parser that outputs a `bun.ast.Expr`.
No env-var substitution, no npm-specific extensions.

**Public interface to expose** (mirrors `src/interchange/toml.zig`):
```zig
pub const INI = struct {
    pub fn parse(
        source:    *const logger.Source,
        log:       *logger.Log,
```

# Plan: Add `Bun.INI` Namespace with `parse` and `stringify`

## Context

Bun already exposes `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML` as
public format-namespace objects. There is an existing *internal* INI parser
(`src/ini.zig`) used only for `.npmrc` processing — it is tied to npm-specific
semantics (env-var substitution, `key[] = value` arrays, `DotEnv.Loader`).

The goal is to expose a new, **self-contained** `Bun.INI` namespace following
the exact same pattern as the existing format objects. The parser should handle
the standard subset: sections → nested objects, `key = value` pairs, and
`;`/`#` comment lines. A stringify method is required too.

**Do NOT import or re-use `src/ini.zig`** — it is the internal npm parser.

---

## Architecture Overview

Every existing format namespace wires through exactly these layers:

```
src/interchange/X.zig            ← standalone parse engine (Expr AST output)
src/interchange.zig              ← re-exports every interchange module
src/bun.js/api/XObject.zig       ← JSC binding (create/parse/stringify)
src/bun.js/api.zig               ← imports XObject
src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig     ← lazy prop + getter fn + @export
src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h  ← FOR_EACH_GETTER macro
src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp        ← bunObjectTable entry
test/js/bun/ini/ini-public.test.ts       ← tests
```

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — `src/interchange/ini.zig` (new file)

Create a self-contained INI parser that outputs a `bun.ast.Expr`.
No env-var substitution, no npm-specific extensions.

**Public interface to expose** (mirrors `src/interchange/toml.zig`):
```zig
pub const INI = struct {
    pub fn parse(
        source:    *const logger.Source,
        log:       *logger.Log,
        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
    ) anyerror!Expr { ... }
};
```

**Parsing algorithm** (line-oriented, no lexer needed):
1. Split input on `\n`; strip `\r` from each line.
2. Skip blank lines and lines whose first non-space char is `;` or `#`.
3. Section header `[name]` (optionally dotted, e.g. `[a.b.c]`):
   - Split `name` on `.`, creating nested `e_object` nodes as needed.
   - Track a `current_section: *E.Object` pointer.
4. Key-value lines `key = value` (also accept `key: value`):
   - Trim whitespace from key and value.
   - Strip inline comments (`;`/`#` after un-quoted content).
   - Parse value: `true`/`false` → `e_boolean`, numeric → `e_number`,
     quoted string → `e_string` (strip quotes), otherwise `e_string`.
   - Insert as property on `current_section`.
5. Return top-level `e_object` Expr.

**`stringify` helper** (Expr → INI text, used by INIObject):

Alternatively, stringify can live in `INIObject.zig` operating on live JS
values directly (see Step 3). No separate AST-level stringify is needed.

### Step 2 — `src/interchange.zig` (update)

Add one line:
```zig
pub const ini = @import("./interchange/ini.zig");
```

Critical file: `src/interchange.zig` (currently 4 lines).

### Step 3 — `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` (new file)

Follow `src/bun.js/api/TOMLObject.zig` for `parse` and
`src/bun.js/api/JSON5Object.zig` for `stringify`.

```zig
pub fn create(globalThis: *jsc.JSGlobalObject) jsc.JSValue {
    const object = JSValue.createEmptyObject(globalThis, 2);
    object.put(globalThis, ZigString.static("parse"),
        jsc.JSFunction.create(globalThis, "parse", parse, 1, .{}));
    object.put(globalThis, ZigString.static("stringify"),
        jsc.JSFunction.create(globalThis, "stringify", stringify, 1, .{}));
    return object;
}
```

**`parse` implementation** — mirrors TOMLObject exactly:
1. `bun.ArenaAllocator` + `ast.ASTMemoryAllocator` scope.
2. `logger.Log.init`; `arguments[0].toSlice`.
3. Call `INI.parse(&source, &log, allocator)`.
4. `js_printer.printJSON` → JSON string in a buffer.
5. `bun.String.borrowUTF8(slice).toJSByParseJSON(globalThis)` → JS value.

**`stringify` implementation** — walks the live JS object recursively:
- Top-level primitive properties are emitted first as `key = value\n`.
- Top-level object-valued properties become `[section]\nkey = value\n…`.
- Nested objects inside a section become dotted section names (e.g. `[a.b]`).
- Value serialisation: `bool` → `true`/`false`, number → decimal string,
  null/undefined → `""`, string → quoted only if it contains `=`, `\n`,
  `;`, or `#`, otherwise bare.
- Use `bun.StringBuilder` to accumulate output; return as `bun.String.toJS`.

Reusable utilities:
- `jsc.JSObject.getOwnPropertyNames` or `jsc.JSObject.forEach` to iterate.
- `bun.StringBuilder` (wtf.StringBuilder) for output accumulation.

### Step 4 — `src/bun.js/api.zig` (update)

Add alongside the other format objects (lines 29–34):
```zig
pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");
```

### Step 5 — `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` (update, 3 locations)

**a) Lazy property declaration** (near line 70, in the `BunObject` struct):
```zig
pub const INI = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getINIObject);
```

**b) Getter function** (near line 1287, with the other `get*Object` fns):
```zig
pub fn getINIObject(globalThis: *jsc.JSGlobalObject, _: *jsc.JSObject) jsc.JSValue {
    return INIObject.create(globalThis);
}
```

**c) Export in `exportAll()`** (near line 140, lazy property callbacks section):
```zig
@export(&BunObject.INI, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("INI") });
```

### Step 6 — `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h` (update)

Add `macro(INI) \` to the `FOR_EACH_GETTER` macro (alphabetically, after
`hash`, before `inspect` — or after `JSONC` to keep format names together):
```cpp
macro(INI) \
```

### Step 7 — `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp` (update)

Inside the `@begin bunObjectTable` … `@end bunObjectTable` block (near line 963),
add after the YAML entry:
```
INI                                            BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI                                       DontDelete|PropertyCallback
```

### Step 8 — Test file `test/js/bun/ini/ini-public.test.ts` (new file)

Use `Bun.INI.parse` / `Bun.INI.stringify` directly (no internal-for-testing).

Key test cases:
```ts
// parse: sections as nested objects
Bun.INI.parse("[section]\nkey = value")
// → { section: { key: "value" } }

// parse: dotted sections
Bun.INI.parse("[a.b]\nkey = val")
// → { a: { b: { key: "val" } } }

// parse: comments
Bun.INI.parse("; comment\n# comment\nkey = value")
// → { key: "value" }

// parse: boolean and number values
Bun.INI.parse("enabled = true\ncount = 42")
// → { enabled: true, count: 42 }

// parse: top-level keys before and after sections
// stringify: roundtrip
const obj = { name: "bun", db: { host: "localhost", port: 5432 } }
Bun.INI.parse(Bun.INI.stringify(obj)) deep-equals obj

// error: non-string argument throws
```

---

## Critical Files to Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/interchange/ini.zig` | **Create** — self-contained INI parser |
| `src/interchange.zig` | **Update** — add `pub const ini` export |
| `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` | **Create** — JSC binding |
| `src/bun.js/api.zig` | **Update** — add `pub const INIObject` import |
| `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` | **Update** — 3 locations (decl, getter, export) |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h` | **Update** — `FOR_EACH_GETTER` macro |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp` | **Update** — `bunObjectTable` entry |
| `test/js/bun/ini/ini-public.test.ts` | **Create** — end-to-end tests |

---

## Existing Utilities to Reuse

| Utility | File | Usage |
|---------|------|-------|
| `bun.ArenaAllocator` | (stdlib wrapper) | Parse-time memory |
| `ast.ASTMemoryAllocator` | `bun.ast` | AST node allocation scope |
| `logger.Log` / `logger.Source` | `bun.logger` | Error collection |
| `js_printer.printJSON` + `BufferPrinter` | `bun.js_printer` | AST → JSON string |

---

  /**
   * INI related APIs
   */
  namespace INI {
    /**
     * Parse an INI string into a JavaScript object.
     *
     * Sections (`[section]`) become nested objects. Dotted section names
     * (`[a.b.c]`) produce deeply nested objects. Values are automatically
     * coerced: `"true"`/`"false"` become booleans, `"null"` becomes `null`,
     * numeric strings become numbers, and everything else stays a string.
     * Lines starting with `;` or `#` are treated as comments.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The INI string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript object
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * const result = INI.parse(`
     * name = Alice
     * age = 30
     *
     * [database]
     * host = localhost
     * port = 5432
     * `);
     * // { name: "Alice", age: 30, database: { host: "localhost", port: 5432 } }
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): object;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI string.
     *
     * Top-level primitive values are written as `key = value` lines.
     * Top-level object values produce `[section]` blocks. Nested objects
     * produce dotted section headers such as `[section.subsection]`.
     * Arrays, symbols, functions, and `undefined` values are silently skipped.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param value The JavaScript object to stringify
     * @returns A string containing the INI document

     * import { INI } from "bun";

     * const result = INI.stringify({
     *   name: "Alice",

     * });
     * // name = Alice
     * //
     * // [database]
     * // host = localhost
     * // port = 5432
     * ```
     */
    export function stringify(value: object): string;
  }
